content:
# Agent Teams Adapter Phase 2: Staging Ingestion -- Decision Record (2026-03-29)

## What
Extended claude-sessions adapter with agent-teams domain mode that reads staging data and emits team structure, task, and message knowledge entries.

## Key Design Decisions
1. **Domain dispatch via source_path**: source_path.contains("agent-teams") routes to teams path. Simple, no protocol changes needed. Two corvia.toml source entries invoke the same binary with different source_path values.
2. **Per-team .ingested writes**: Originally batched all writes to end of run. 5-persona review caught crash-safety issue. Changed to per-team append immediately after emission. Failed config teams are NOT marked ingested so they retry on next run.
3. **Timestamp-aware status inference**: infer_team_status uses HashMap with (event, timestamp) tuples. Compares timestamps when deciding latest event per task_id. Fixes latent bug where flock-serialized but temporally-unordered events could misclassify team status.
4. **content_role "task" maps to Procedural**: Added to MemoryType::try_from_role. Procedural fits because tasks are work items (action-oriented), not structural or decisional knowledge.
5. **Broadcast dedup by hash(sender+content+timestamp)**: Intentionally excludes recipient so same-sender/same-content/same-timestamp across multiple inboxes deduplicates to one message. Different senders with identical content preserved.

## Review Findings Worth Remembering
- read_dir() never yields . or .. entries on any Rust-supported platform. No need to filter them.
- HashMap insertion order != temporal order. Always use explicit timestamp comparison when "latest event" semantics are needed.
- Per-entry state writes (append to .ingested after each team) provide crash-safe idempotency at negligible I/O cost.
- Teams with unparseable config should NOT be marked as ingested. They may have temporarily corrupt files that will be valid on retry.
- Inbox read_dir ordering is nondeterministic. Sort by filename before dedup to ensure deterministic "winning" recipient on broadcast messages.

## What Blocked / Surprised
- Nothing blocked. RFC was thorough and pre-reviewed.
- The number of clippy lint fixes needed was higher than expected (6 collapsible-if warnings, 1 cloned-ref-to-slice-refs). Edition 2024 let-chains help but clippy is strict about nesting.
